target.name: Shield AI
target.founder: Brandon Tseng, Ryan Tseng, Andrew Reiter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $2.2B - $78.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Shield AI, a Series F defense & security company that has raised $1.6B+, is valued at $5.6B post-money. The relevant comp universe comprises 10 peers (2 public, 8 private) in the Defense & Security sector at same or adjacent funding stages, including Anduril Industries, Flock Safety, and Skydio.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.3x multiplier typical of Series F-to-next-stage advancement to the $5.6B base, yielding $7.3B. Peer-median triangulation across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Anduril at $78.0B Series G, Flock Safety at $7.5B Series E, Skydio at $2.2B Series E) produces a median of $7.5B, though the range of $2.2B–$78.0B reflects meaningful dispersion around stage and business-model maturity.

Synthesizing both methods yields a triangulated range of $6.2B (low) to $8.6B (high), with a median of $7.4B. This implies meaningful upside from the current $5.6B valuation, though the wide peer range—driven primarily by Anduril's significantly higher Series G valuation—signals material uncertainty around comparable precedent. Key risks include stage-specific market appetite, sector regulatory shifts, and the heterogeneity of defense-tech business models and commercialization timelines.
